vision: A mobile app that helps individuals manage their daily tasks through simple task creation, completion tracking, and organisation without the complexity of full project management tools.

epics.name: Task Creation & Management
epics.description: Core functionality allowing users to create, edit, and delete tasks to maintain their personal task list

stories.title: Create a new task with title
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to create a new task with a title, so that I can capture items I need to do
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the task list screen When I tap the add task button and enter a title and save Then a new task appears in my task list with the entered title
Given I am creating a new task When I leave the title field empty and attempt to save Then I see a validation error and the task is not created
Given I am creating a new task When I enter a title and save Then the task is created with incomplete status by default
stories.notes: Foundation story - establishes core data model and basic UI

stories.title: Add optional due date to task
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to add an optional due date when creating a task, so that I can track when tasks need to be completed
acceptance_criteria: Given I am creating a new task When I tap the due date field Then I see a date picker to select a date
Given I am creating a task When I select a due date and save Then the task displays the due date in the task list
Given I am creating a task When I do not select a due date and save Then the task is created successfully without a due date

stories.title: Delete a task from the list
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to delete a task from my list, so that I can remove items that are no longer relevant
acceptance_criteria: Given I am viewing my task list When I swipe on a task and tap delete Then the task is removed from my list
Given I tap delete on a task When I confirm the deletion Then the task is permanently removed and cannot be recovered
Given I tap delete on a task When I cancel the deletion Then the task remains in my list unchanged
stories.notes: Include confirmation to prevent accidental deletion

stories.title: Edit an existing task
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to edit an existing task, so that I can correct mistakes or update task details
acceptance_criteria: Given I am viewing my task list When I tap on a task Then I see an edit screen with the current task title and due date
Given I am editing a task When I change the title and save Then the task list displays the updated title
Given I am editing a task When I change or remove the due date and save Then the task reflects the updated due date

epics.name: Task Completion
epics.description: Functionality enabling users to mark tasks as complete to track their progress through daily activities

stories.title: Mark a task as complete
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to mark a task as complete, so that I can track my progress and see what I have accomplished
acceptance_criteria: Given I am viewing my task list When I tap the completion checkbox on an incomplete task Then the task is marked as complete with visual indication
Given a task is marked complete When I view the task list Then the completed task displays with a strikethrough or dimmed style
Given a task is marked complete When I tap the completion checkbox again Then the task is marked as incomplete
stories.notes: Core interaction - should be quick and satisfying

epics.name: Task List Viewing
epics.description: Display functionality allowing users to view their tasks in an organised list format

stories.title: View all tasks in list format
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to view all my tasks in a list format, so that I can see everything I need to do at a glance
acceptance_criteria: Given I have tasks saved When I open the app Then I see all my tasks displayed in a scrollable list
Given I have no tasks saved When I open the app Then I see an empty state message encouraging me to add a task
Given I have tasks with due dates When I view the list Then each task displays its title and due date if present
stories.notes: Walking skeleton - core screen that ties everything together

epics.name: Task Filtering
epics.description: Filtering capabilities allowing users to focus on specific subsets of their tasks based on completion status

stories.title: Filter to show only incomplete tasks
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to filter my tasks to show only incomplete items, so that I can focus on what still needs to be done
acceptance_criteria: Given I am viewing my task list When I select the incomplete tasks filter Then I see only tasks that are not marked complete
Given I have the incomplete filter active When I mark a task as complete Then that task is removed from the current view
Given I have the incomplete filter active When there are no incomplete tasks Then I see a message indicating all tasks are complete

stories.title: Filter to show only completed tasks
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to filter my tasks to show only completed items, so that I can review what I have accomplished
acceptance_criteria: Given I am viewing my task list When I select the completed tasks filter Then I see only tasks that are marked complete
Given I have the completed filter active When I mark a task as incomplete Then that task is removed from the current view
Given I have the completed filter active When there are no completed tasks Then I see a message indicating no tasks have been completed yet
stories.notes: Lower priority than incomplete filter as primary use case is seeing what needs to be done

epics.name: Task Sorting
epics.description: Sorting capabilities allowing users to organise their task list by due date to prioritise upcoming deadlines

stories.title: Sort tasks by due date
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to sort my tasks by due date, so that I can see upcoming deadlines first and prioritise accordingly
acceptance_criteria: Given I am viewing my task list When I select sort by due date Then tasks are ordered with earliest due dates at the top
Given I have tasks with and without due dates When I sort by due date Then tasks without due dates appear at the bottom of the list
Given I am sorting by due date When I have multiple tasks with the same due date Then those tasks maintain their relative order

epics.name: Data Persistence
epics.description: Local storage functionality ensuring user tasks are saved and persist between app sessions

stories.title: Persist tasks locally on device
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want my tasks to be saved locally on my device, so that I do not lose my task list when I close the app
acceptance_criteria: Given I have created tasks When I close and reopen the app Then all my previously created tasks are still visible
Given I have marked tasks as complete When I close and reopen the app Then the completion status of each task is preserved
Given I have edited a task When I close and reopen the app Then the edited task details are preserved
stories.notes: Critical infrastructure - required for walking skeleton

stories.title: Handle up to 100 tasks performantly
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want the app to load my task list quickly even with many tasks, so that I can access my tasks without delay
acceptance_criteria: Given I have 100 tasks saved When I open the app Then the task list loads and displays within 1 second
Given I have 100 tasks displayed When I scroll through the list Then the scrolling is smooth without lag or stuttering
Given I have 100 tasks When I apply a filter Then the filtered results display within 1 second
stories.notes: Performance optimisation - verify after core features complete

epics.name: Cross-Platform Support
epics.description: Mobile platform availability ensuring the app works on both iOS and Android devices

stories.title: Access app on iOS device
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to use the app on my iOS device, so that I can manage my tasks on my iPhone or iPad
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an iOS device When I download the app from the App Store Then the app installs and launches successfully
Given I am using the app on iOS When I interact with all features Then they function correctly following iOS design patterns
Given I am using the app on iOS When I view the interface Then it displays correctly on various iPhone screen sizes

stories.title: Access app on Android device
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to use the app on my Android device, so that I can manage my tasks on my Android phone or tablet
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an Android device When I download the app from the Play Store Then the app installs and launches successfully
Given I am using the app on Android When I interact with all features Then they function correctly following Android design patterns
Given I am using the app on Android When I view the interface Then it displays correctly on various Android screen sizes
